ablation_conclusions: MiniLM FP16 GPU equals MiniLM INT8 CPU on R@k (98.0 vs 98.2 is noise) — GPU buys 18x speed without changing recall.
v0.14.x retrieval policy does not improve recall in isolation.
The +1.0pp R@5 lift to 99.2% comes from (a) switching to e5-base and (b) enabling reranker. Run 1 vs run 2 isolates the reranker contribution: +4.2pp R@1, +1.2pp R@5.
Reranker is the dominant factor, not architecture changes.
notes: No FP16 version of bge-reranker available; reranker INT8 ops fall back to CPU even with CUDA EP registered. That is why rerank=on is 2.4x slower than rerank=off.
Honest framing for v1.0 release notes: this headline number is what the model + reranker config delivers; the validity-model architecture changes (v1.4-v1.6) need their own R@k regression check.
v1.0 release used these numbers as the comparison baseline; v1.4-v1.6 calibration is tracked in issue #16.
